channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Humpback whale sets migration record with 15,000-kilometer journey
item.description: Researchers have documented two humpback whales traveling between breeding areas in Australia and...

item.title: Scientists use fecal DNA to rescue the world's rarest marsupial
item.description: Australian scientists are employing advanced DNA methods to protect the critically endangered Gilbert's potoroo, one of the rarest marsupials.

item.title: Rainforest discovery challenges long-held views on early human migration
item.description: New evidence shows ancient humans lived in rainforests 150,000 years ago, contradicting earlier beliefs.

item.title: T. rex's small arms may have evolved due to its massive head
item.description: Researchers propose that T. rex's tiny arms resulted from its oversized head becoming the primary hunting tool.
